content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
February 9, 2017 
Diane Patterson 
Assistant Clerk of the Board 
County of Marin 
Board of Supervisors - Clerk of the Board 
3501 Civic Center Drive, Suite 329 
San Rafael, CA 94903 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-17-012 
Dear Ms. Patterson: 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of the Marin County Board of 
Supervisors regarding the conflict of interest code provisions of the Political Reform Act (the 
"Act").1 Because your inquiry is of a general nature, we are treating your request as one for 
infonnal assistance. 2 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the Act. We are not a finder of fact 
when rendering advice (111 re Oglesby (1975) l FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes 
your facts are complete and accurate. 
QUESTION 
Is a county "special district," "miscellaneous district," or joint powers authority entity 
("JPA") a "local government agency" under the Act and therefore required to submit a Conflict of 
Interest Code to the County Board of Supervisors for review? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes, these entities meet the definition of a "local government agency" set forth in Section 
82041, and those entities wholly within the county must submit a Conflict of Interest Code for 
review by the County Board of Supervisors. (Section 8201 l(b).) 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 
! lnfonnal assistance does not provide the requester with the immunity provided by an opinion or fonnal 
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation !8329(c)(3).) 


FACTS 
File No. 1-17-012 
Page No. 2 
You are the Assistant Clerk to the Marin County Board of Supervisors. You seek guidance 
in order to complete your list oflocal agencies required to submit a Conflict oflnterest Code to the 
County Board of Supervisors for review for the upcoming 2018 Biennial Notice period. 
You state that the County Local Agency Formation Commission ("LAFCO") has developed 
a ''Directory of Marin County Local Governments" which includes "Community Services Districts, 
Fire Protection Districts, Public Utility Districts, Sanitary Districts, Water Districts, County Service 
Areas, School Districts," and such. Most of these entities are currently on your County Biennial 
Notice requirement contact list. Agencies with jurisdiction in more than one county are not on your 
contact list. 
Your concern is whether "Miscellaneous Districts," "County-Governed Special Districts," 
other "special districts," and various JP As within Marin County, such as the "Marin County Animal 
Control Program," that have not been consistently part of the County Biennial Notice, should also 
be considered local agencies and subject to the requirement to submit a Conflict of Interest Code to 
the County. 
ANALYSIS 
Under the Act, every agency is required to adopt and promulgate a Conflict of Interest Code, 
which sets forth appropriate financial disclosure categories for public officials within an agency 
who engage in governmental decisionrnaking. (Sections 87300 and 87302.) An "agency" is defined 
as "any state agency or local government agency." (Section 82003.) A "local government agency" 
is defined for purposes of the Act as: 
" . . .  a county, city or district of any kind including school district, or any other 
local or regional political subdivision, or any department, division, bureau, office, 
board, commission or other agency of the foregoing." 
( Section 82041, emphasis added.) 
The entities you inquire about are clearly of a public nature, and meet the definition stated in 
Section 82041. The miscellaneous and special districts for the County are county "districts" and 
qualify as a "local government agency" under Section 82041. A JP A is defined by statute as 
consisting of "two or more public agencies" authorized to jointly exercise power. (Sections 6500 
and 6502, emphasis added.) Therefore, a JP A meets the definition of a "local government agency" 
under Section 82041as well. (See also Magill Advice Letter, No. A-12-083; Watts Advice Letter, 
No. A-03-258; Weiss Advice Letter, A-01-122; Crabb Advice Letter, No. A-97-575, Siegel Advice 
Letter, No. A-81-015; and Hopkins Advice Letter, No. A-77-274.) 
Although your inquiry does not raise the issue, a nonprofit corporation may also meet the 
definition of a local government agency if it meets certain factors establishing that its "overall 
character" is that of a local government agency. (In re Siegel (1977) 3 FPPC Ops. 62, Rasiah 
Advice Letter, No. A-01-020.) When entities are clearly public, it is not necessary to apply the 


File No. l -17-012 
Page No. 3 
Siegel criteria to detennine that the entity is a public agency within the meaning of the Act. (Siegel 
Advice Letter, supra, p. 2) Also, please note that agencies that are detennined to be "solely 
advisory" are not required to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code, as the definition of "public official" 
does not include individuals who perfonn duties as part of a body that does not exercise decision­
making authority. (Regulation 18700(c)(2), and see Kendig Advice Letter, No. A-15-182.) We 
advise you to contact our office if you require additional advice relating to a particular advisory 
body or nonprofit corporation. 
Additional Information regarding Local Agency Conflict of Interest Codes 
Local agencies are required to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code designating those positions 
within the agency that involve the making of governmental decisions and requiring officials and 
employees holding those positions to file a Fonn 700 (Statement of Economic Interests) disclosing 
certain financial interests. (Sections 87300 and 87302; Miles Advice Letter, No. A-05-162.) The 
Marin County Board of Supervisors is the code reviewing body for Marin County agencies and any 
other local government agency whose jurisdiction is determined to be wholly within the county. 3 
(Section 8201 l(b).) The Commission is the code reviewing body for any agency with jurisdiction in 
more than one county. (Section 8201 l(a).) 
The Commission may provide technical assistance to a party in order to detennine whether 
that party is an agency as defined in Sections 82041, and is therefore required to adopt and 
promulgate a Conflict oflnterest Code pursuant to Section 87300. (Regulation 18329.S(c).) The 
Commission may also provide advice or assistance to an agency concerning which positions should 
be designated in the agency's Conflict of Interest Code. Local agencies may contact the 
Commission for formal or informal advice as to their status by emailing advicc@fppc.ca.gov. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
LKH:jgl 
3 This excludes any city agency. (Section 820 I t(b).) 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
By: 
L. Karen Harrison 
Counsel, Legal Division

written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation !8329(c)(3).)
